page: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU virtual server instance powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, configured with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs via multithreading at two threads per core. The system is equipped with 125.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 3.91 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 600 GB SSD, and the instance does not include any GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test and PassMark CPU score placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic capabilities are also strong, as shown by the PassMark CPU encryption test. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score in the top 25%, while uncached read and write memory bandwidths average 84.57 GB/sec and 62.84 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance, static web serving throughput, and database operations fall within the average range. For language model workloads, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 360.07 tokens/sec on small 135M models, but performance drops to a poor rating of 2.36 tokens/sec for large 70B models.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores and balanced resource allocation, the ccx53 offers a highly stable compute profile without the resource contention of shared-core instances. This makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance. The server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, medium-scale database operations, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU instance featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 125.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the QEMU hypervisor, it includes 600 GB of local SSD storage and no GPU. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and cryptographic execution, all placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with mid-range averages. The instance excels at small-scale LLM text generation, reaching top-tier speeds of 360.07 tokens/sec, but struggles with large 70B models. With dedicated CPU allocation and a balanced memory-to-core ratio, the ccx53 provides a stable, cost-efficient platform for software compilation, web serving, database management, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated AMD EPYC Milan instance with 32 vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance and balanced general-purpose compute.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated AMD EPYC Milan server with 32 vCPUs, 125 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server featuring thirty-two virtual cores and balanced memory for predictable, multi-tenant-free general-purpose computing.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC Milan processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
125.0 GB of system memory paired with 600 GB of local SSD storage.
Strong single-core CPU, cryptographic, and memory benchmark performance.
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language models.
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and database operations.
